channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: "I felt guilty for being the chosen one": Ada Ferrer on her memoir about leaving Cuba
item.description: When she fled Cuba, Ada Ferrer's mother took only one of her two children. In her new memoir, Keeper of My Kin, Ferrer grapples with that decision's reverberations across generations of her family.
item.content:encoded: When she fled Cuba, Ada Ferrer's mother took only one of her two children. In her new memoir, Keeper of My Kin, Ferrer grapples with that decision's reverberations across generations of her family.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for May 30, 2026: Our Endless Summer with Tiffany Haddish, Lucy Dacus, and more!
item.description: This week, we celebrate an early start to summer by revisiting our interviews with Tiffany Haddish, Taimane, Becca Mann, and Lucy Dacus!
item.content:encoded:
This week, we celebrate an early start to summer by revisiting our interviews with Tiffany Haddish, Taimane, Becca Mann, and Lucy Dacus!

(Image credit: Emma McIntyre)

item.title: Advice giving has become a booming industry. Is it working?
item.description: From bestselling books to popular podcasts, advice giving has become an industry. But do professional advice-givers really solve people's problems?
item.content:encoded: From bestselling books to popular podcasts, advice giving has become an industry. But do professional advice-givers really solve people's problems?
item.dc:creator: Justin Kramon

item.title: Trump's name must come off of the Kennedy Center, judge rules
item.description: The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now
item.content:encoded:
added to the Kennedy Center in December of 2025. This photo was taken in April 2026.'/>

The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: John C. Reilly talks about how being a middle child led to his acting career
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. John C. Reilly talks about how being a middle child led to his acting career.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. John C. Reilly talks about how being a middle child led to his acting career.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: Back from Cannes, a critic shares the films he's most excited to see again
item.description: Though the 2026 festival featured less Hollywood razzle-dazzle than in years past, there were still plenty of great films. Most notable: All of a Sudden, from the Japanese director Ryûsuke Hamaguchi.
item.content:encoded: Though the 2026 festival featured less Hollywood razzle-dazzle than in years past, there were still plenty of great films. Most notable: All of a Sudden, from the Japanese director Ryûsuke Hamaguchi.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: The 'Hacks' finale ties a melodramatic bow onto a beloved series
item.description: The final episode of the HBO Max comedy added an extra emotional punch. After five seasons, it wasn't quite necessary.
item.content:encoded:
The final episode of the HBO Max comedy added an extra emotional punch. After five seasons, it wasn't quite necessary.

(Image credit: HBO Max)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: Meteorologists were central to D-Day. 'Pressure' tells the story of navigating uncertainty
item.description: The new movie, based on writer and actor David Haig's 2014 play, dramatizes the tensions between military leaders and meteorologists in the lead up to the Allied invasion of Normandy.
item.content:encoded:
The new movie, based on writer and actor David Haig's 2014 play, dramatizes the tensions between military leaders and meteorologists in the lead up to the Allied invasion of Normandy.

(Image credit: Alex Bailey)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Jonah Wolpert

item.title: Photos show the world's 'uncounted' people -- and what it takes to be counted
item.description: Colombian-American photographer and filmmaker Juan Arredondo turns his lens on the people of the world who do not have birth and death certificates — and how these vital records are created.
item.content:encoded:
Colombian-American photographer and filmmaker Juan Arredondo turns his lens on the people of the world who do not have birth and death certificates — and how these vital records are created.

(Image credit: Juan Arredondo)
item.dc:creator: Diane Cole

item.title: Why the U.S. cattle herd is at a 75-year low — and what it means for beef prices
item.description: The overall number of U.S. beef and dairy cattle has shrunk to its lowest level since 1951. Drought, rising operating costs and increased consolidation are among the causes.
item.content:encoded:
The overall number of U.S. beef and dairy cattle has shrunk to its lowest level since 1951. Drought, rising operating costs and increased consolidation are among the causes.

(Image credit: Luke Sharrett for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez
